title: Plasmid DNA Extraction Protocol
author: Shuning Guo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.byqzpvx6

steps.title: Solate a single colony from a...
steps.instruction: Solate a single colony from a freshly streaked selective plate, and inoculate a culture of 1- 5 mL LB medium containing the appropriate selective antibiotic. Incubate for ~12-16 hours at 37°C with vigorous shaking (~ 300 rpm). Use a 10-20 mL culture tube or a flask with a volume of at least 4 times the volume of the culture. It is strongly recommended that an endA negative strain of E. coli be used for routine plasmid isolation. Examples of such strains include DH5a® and JM109®.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute at room temperature. 10000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Decant or aspirate and discard the culture media
steps.instruction: Decant or aspirate and discard the culture media.

steps.title: Add 250 µL Solution I/RNase A
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL Solution I/RNase A. Vortex or pipet up and down to mix thoroughly. Complete resuspension of cell pellet is vital for obtaining good yields. RNase A must be added to Solution I before use.

steps.title: Transfer suspension into a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer suspension into a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 250 µL Solution II
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL Solution II. Invert and gently rotate the tube several times to obtain a clear lysate. A 2-3 minute incubation may be necessary. Avoid vigorous mixing as this will shear chromosomal DNA and lower plasmid purity. Do not allow the lysis reaction to proceed more than 5 minutes. Store Solution II tightly capped when not in use to avoid acidification from CO2 in the air.

steps.title: Add 350 µL Solution III
steps.instruction: Add 350 µL Solution III. Immediately invert several times until a flocculent white precipitate forms. It is vital that the solution is mixed thoroughly and immediately after the addition of Solution III to avoid localized precipitation.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed (≥13,000 x g) for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed (≥13,000 x g) for 10 minutes. A compact white pellet will form. Promptly proceed to the next step. 15000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes

steps.title: Insert a HiBind® DNA Mini Column...
steps.instruction: Insert a HiBind® DNA Mini Column into a 2 mL Collection Tube.

steps.title: Transfer the cleared supernatant from Step...
steps.instruction: Transfer the cleared supernatant from Step 8 by CAREFULLY aspirating it into the HiBind® DNA Mini Column. Be careful not to disturb the pellet and that no cellular debris is transferred to the HiBind® DNA Mini Column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute. 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the filtrate and reuse the collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate and reuse the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL HBC Buffer HBC...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL HBC Buffer HBC Buffer must be diluted with isopropanol before use. Please see Page 6 for instructions.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute. 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the filtrate and reuse collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate and reuse collection tube.

steps.title: Add 700 µL DNA Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL DNA Wash Buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute. 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the filtrate and reuse the collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate and reuse the collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the empty HiBind® DNA Mini...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the empty HiBind® DNA Mini Column for 2 minutes at maximum speed to dry the column matrix.

steps.title: Transfer the HiBind® DNA Mini Column to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the HiBind® DNA Mini Column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 30-100 μL Elution Buffer or...
steps.instruction: Add 30-100 μL Elution Buffer or sterile deionized water directly to the center of the column membrane. The efficiency of eluting DNA from the HiBind® DNA Mini Column is dependent on pH. If using sterile deionized water, make sure that the pH is around 8.5.

steps.title: Let sit at room temperature for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Let sit at room temperature for 1 minute.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 1 minute. 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute This represents approximately 70% of bound DNA. An optional second elution will yield any residual DNA, though at a lower concentration.

steps.title: Test the concentration and purity of DNA using NanoDrop
steps.instruction: Test the concentration and purity of DNA using NanoDrop.

steps.title: Store DNA at -20°C
steps.instruction: Store DNA at -20°C.

steps.title: Repeat step 16~18 once
steps.instruction: Repeat step 16~18 once.

steps.title: Suck out the solution from the...
steps.instruction: Suck out the solution from the tube and re-add it to the center of the column membrane to give a second centrifuge. 15000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
